annotations.label: Hamiltonian mechanics

anchor.snippet: Hamiltonian mechanics is a reformulation of Lagrangian mechanics

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formalization of Hamiltonian mechanics as a framework.

annotations.label: Generalized momenta

anchor.section: Phase space coordinates ( p , q ) and Hamiltonian H
anchor.snippet: are called momenta

annotations.note: No notion of generalized momenta (∂L/∂q̇) is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Legendre transformation of the Lagrangian

anchor.section: Phase space coordinates ( p , q ) and Hamiltonian H
anchor.snippet: the Legendre transformation of

annotations.note: The Legendre transform is not defined in Mathlib (the Legendre files concern the symbol, not the transform).

annotations.label: Energy function

anchor.section: Phase space coordinates ( p , q ) and Hamiltonian H
anchor.snippet: defines the energy function

annotations.note: No Lagrangian energy function E_L = q̇·∂L/∂q̇ − L is defined.

annotations.label: Hamiltonian

anchor.section: Phase space coordinates ( p , q ) and Hamiltonian H
anchor.snippet: known as the Hamiltonian

annotations.note: Mathlib has no definition of a (classical-mechanics) Hamiltonian function.

annotations.label: Phase space coordinates

anchor.section: Phase space coordinates ( p , q ) and Hamiltonian H
anchor.snippet: is called phase space coordinates

annotations.note: Phase space (p, q) coordinates are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler–Lagrange becomes Hamilton's equations

anchor.section: From Euler–Lagrange equation to Hamilton's equations
anchor.snippet: becomes Hamilton's equations

annotations.note: Neither the Euler–Lagrange equation nor Hamilton's equations appear in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stationary action characterization of Hamilton's equations

anchor.section: From stationary action principle to Hamilton's equations
anchor.snippet: obeys the Hamilton equations

annotations.note: The action principle yielding Hamilton's equations is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: One-dimensional nonrelativistic particle

anchor.snippet: A simple interpretation of Hamiltonian mechanics

annotations.note: No formalization of a physical particle Hamiltonian in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Spherical pendulum

anchor.snippet: A spherical pendulum consists of a mass

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize the spherical pendulum example.

annotations.label: Equivalence of Lagrange's and Hamilton's equations

anchor.snippet: Thus Lagrange's equations are equivalent to Hamilton's equations

annotations.note: Equivalence of Lagrange and Hamilton equations is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hamiltonian equals total energy iff energy function does

anchor.snippet: is the total energy of the system if and only if the energy function

annotations.note: Not present: no Hamiltonian or energy-function notion in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Invariance of Hamiltonian under point transformations

anchor.snippet: does not change under point transformations

annotations.note: Point-transformation invariance of the Hamiltonian is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cyclic (ignorable) coordinate

anchor.snippet: A coordinate for which the last equation holds is called cyclic

annotations.note: No notion of a cyclic/ignorable coordinate exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conditions for Hamiltonian to equal T + V

anchor.section: Hamiltonian as the total system energy
anchor.snippet: The relation holds true for nonrelativistic systems

annotations.note: Conditions for H = T + V are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kinetic energy of a system of N point masses

anchor.section: Application to systems of point masses
anchor.snippet: Consider the kinetic energy for a system of N point masses

annotations.note: No N-body kinetic energy formula is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conservation of Hamiltonian implies conservation of energy

anchor.snippet: conservation of the Hamiltonian implies conservation of energy

annotations.note: Energy conservation along Hamiltonian flow is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hamiltonian of a charged particle in an electromagnetic field

anchor.section: Hamiltonian of a charged particle in an electromagnetic field
anchor.snippet: A sufficient illustration of Hamiltonian mechanics

annotations.note: No electromagnetic Hamiltonian is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Symplectic 2-form on the phase manifold

anchor.snippet: As a closed nondegenerate symplectic 2-form

annotations.note: Mathlib has the symplectic group/bilinear form but no symplectic 2-form on a smooth manifold.

annotations.label: Darboux's theorem (canonical coordinates)

anchor.snippet: According to Darboux's theorem

annotations.note: Mathlib's Darboux is the calculus intermediate-value theorem for derivatives (Mathlib/Analysis/Calculus/Darboux.lean), not the symplectic-geometry Darboux theorem.

annotations.label: Hamiltonian vector field

anchor.snippet: is known as a Hamiltonian vector field

annotations.note: Hamiltonian vector fields are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hamilton's equation (geometric)

anchor.snippet: is called Hamilton's equation

annotations.note: The geometric form ι_{X_H}ω = dH is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hamiltonian system as fiber bundle

anchor.snippet: A Hamiltonian system may be understood as a fiber bundle

annotations.note: Mathlib has fiber bundles but no Hamiltonian-system bundle construction.

annotations.label: Phase space (symplectic manifold)

anchor.snippet: is then called the phase space

annotations.note: Symplectic manifolds are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hamiltonian flow

anchor.snippet: induces a Hamiltonian flow on the manifold

annotations.note: Hamiltonian flow is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Liouville's theorem (symplectomorphisms preserve volume)

anchor.snippet: each symplectomorphism preserves the volume form

annotations.note: Mathlib's Liouville theorems are about bounded entire functions and ODE transcendentals, not symplectic volume preservation.

annotations.label: Poisson bracket of functions

anchor.snippet: it is called a Poisson bracket of functions

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks a Poisson bracket on smooth functions; only general Lie/Poisson-algebra structures exist.

annotations.label: Liouville's theorem (convective derivative vanishes)

anchor.snippet: This is called Liouville's theorem

annotations.note: The phase-space Liouville theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Liouville integrable Hamiltonian

anchor.snippet: the Hamiltonian is Liouville integrable

annotations.note: Liouville integrability is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Liouville–Arnold theorem

anchor.snippet: The Liouville–Arnold theorem says that

annotations.note: The Liouville–Arnold theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quadratic-form (Riemannian) Hamiltonian

anchor.snippet: An important special case consists of those Hamiltonians that are quadratic forms

annotations.note: Mathlib has Riemannian manifolds but no quadratic-form Hamiltonian construction.

annotations.label: Sub-Riemannian manifold

anchor.snippet: one has a sub-Riemannian manifold

annotations.note: Sub-Riemannian manifolds are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sub-Riemannian Hamiltonian

anchor.snippet: is known as a sub-Riemannian Hamiltonian

annotations.note: Sub-Riemannian Hamiltonians are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chow–Rashevskii theorem

anchor.snippet: is given by the Chow–Rashevskii theorem

annotations.note: The Chow–Rashevskii theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Heisenberg group as sub-Riemannian manifold

anchor.snippet: Heisenberg group provides a simple example

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Heisenberg group, let alone its sub-Riemannian structure.

annotations.label: Hamiltonian mechanics on a Poisson algebra

anchor.snippet: Hamiltonian mechanics can be formulated on general commutative unital real Poisson algebras

annotations.note: No Poisson-algebra formulation of Hamiltonian mechanics in Mathlib.

annotations.label: State on a Poisson algebra

anchor.snippet: A state is a continuous linear functional

annotations.note: States on Poisson algebras are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: General Poisson-bracket form of Hamilton's equation

anchor.section: Generalization to quantum mechanics through Poisson bracket
anchor.snippet: the more general form of the Hamilton's equation

annotations.note: The Poisson-bracket form df/dt = {f, H} is not present in Mathlib.
